lyrics:
Cracked ground, open sky
Waited years for a reason why
Dry roots, empty hands
Still I stood on the endless sand

Then the wind turned soft and strange
Smell of hope on the edge of change

Here comes the rain - no intention at all
I open my arms and hope for the fall
Could wash me clean or carry me far
Or just show me what you really are
Here comes the rain - I won't run this time
I'll face what falls and call it mine

Clouds swell in the rising dust
Old thoughts buried start to rust
Truth comes slow as the flood begins
It doesn't ask - it just rushes in

And every drop a choice I make
To build again or let it break

Here comes the rain - no intention at all
No promise to rise, no warning call
Could drown the past or be a spark
Of something growing in the dark
Here comes the rain - I'll let it fall
Whatever it brings, I'll take it all

It's not the rain that writes the tale
It's what I think of storm and hail
Not what it takes, but what remains
Not what it breaks, but what sustains

Here comes the rain - no intention at all
No curse, no crown, no rise, no fall
Could wash me clean or leave a scar
Either way, I've come this far
Here comes the rain - I'm still here
And every drop is crystal clear

Here comes the rain - no intention at all
persona:
## STAB

STAB isn’t just a name-it’s a statement. A sonic force of nature, she channels the electric pulse of '80s pop-rock, fusing jangly guitars, driving basslines, and tight harmonies into anthems that hit hard and leave a mark. Bold, confident, and unapologetically fierce, STAB delivers high-energy hooks with a rebellious edge, embracing the raw, unfiltered spirit of rock.

Her music is a love letter to the golden age of girl bands, blending the infectious melodies of The Bangles with the gritty attitude of The Runaways. Whether she’s tearing through anthemic choruses, throwing down sharp riffs, or tapping into her softer, more introspective side, STAB’s voice is unmistakable-powerful yet effortlessly cool.

A signature artist under Maricopa Records, STAB thrives in the space where analog warmth meets AI-driven creativity, pushing boundaries while staying true to the timeless essence of rock 'n' roll. She’s not here to follow trends-she’s here to carve her own path, one catchy, defiant, and undeniably addictive track at a time.

If you’re looking for music that hits like a lightning bolt and stays in your head for days-STAB is already in your veins.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/7zZ7MxU20RZAw2E1w4EsaE)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/stab/1495050512)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCQkfMbYRs06q_SFW5PPSvWw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/STAB.jpg)
## Releases

impression.text: This track establishes an atmosphere of determined, anthemic rock, balancing a feeling of weary resilience in the verses with powerful hope in the choruses. The energy builds dynamically from a steady, driving feel into an explosive, wide-open chorus, creating a powerful sense of emotional release. The production is polished and spacious, with clean, upfront female vocals sitting comfortably atop a dense wall of layered guitars and a punchy, centered rhythm section. A defining quality of the track is the powerful transition into the chorus at 0:46, where the arrangement swells to create a stadium-sized hook that perfectly captures the song's theme of facing a challenge head-on.

review.review_text: Written in waltz time at a measured 107 bpm, 'Here Comes the Rain' is the kind of song that earns its big moment by making you wait for it. The verses sit in dry, sparse A minor, STAB's voice steady and unadorned over a restrained arrangement, so when the chorus swells at that first drop, the release genuinely lands. The lyric does the heavy lifting without overselling it: 'Could wash me clean or carry me far / Or just show me what you really are' is the sort of line that sounds simple until you realize you've been thinking about it all day. This is STAB working in a more reflective register than usual, closer to early Sheryl Crow than The Runaways, and she pulls it off with enough conviction to make the stadium-sized production feel earned rather than borrowed.
anchors_used: quoted lyric: 'Could wash me clean or carry me far / Or just show me what you really are'
